options.name: Conventional Subgrade + Topsoil
options.description: Compacted subgrade with a standard depth of imported topsoil in planting areas.
cons: Higher long-term plant stress and replacement

options.name: Targeted Soil Improvement
options.description: Selective decompaction and compost amendment in planting and stormwater areas.

options.name: Soil as Infrastructure
options.description: Engineered soils designed to support stormwater infiltration, tree growth, and long-term carbon storage.
pros: Maximizes performance of all landscape systems
Extends lifespan of trees and plantings

options.name: Code-minimum compliance
options.description: Stormwater is managed primarily through a centralized detention basin sized to meet local requirements.
cons: Larger land area dedicated to detention
Limited water quality or site-scale benefits

options.name: Distributed Stormwater Treatment
options.description: Stormwater is managed through a combination of smaller rain gardens, amended soils, and a reduced detention basin.
pros: Reduces basin size and grading impacts
cons: Requires early coordination with civil engineering

options.name: Green Infrastructure first approach
options.description: Stormwater is treated and infiltrated across the site using integrated green infrastructure systems before reaching detention or discharge points.
pros: Maximizes water quality and runoff reduction
Supports long-term resilience and regulatory flexibility
Enhances site character and user experience

options.name: Code-Required Planting
options.description: Plants meet minimum ordinance requirements, typically after site layout is finalized.

options.name: Preservation-First + Strategic Canopy
options.description: Existing healthy trees are prioritized, and new trees are placed to maximize shade and long-term growth.
pros: Improves user comfort and heat mitigation

options.name: Canopy-Driven Site Design
options.description: Tree canopy goals guide site layout, soil volumes, and circulation from the beginning of design.
pros: Longest tree lifespan and lowest replacement rate

options.name: Turf and Ornamental Planting
options.description: Turfgrass with conventional ornamental planting in accent areas.

options.name: Structured Native Planting
options.description: Native and adapted plant species arranged in clear, maintainable planting patterns.

options.name: Habitat-Forward Landscape
options.description: Plant communities are designed to function as habitat while remaining legible and intentional.

options.name: Standard Concrete and Asphalt
options.description: Conventional paving materials sized to minimum standards.

options.name: Material Reduction + Durability Focus
options.description: Reduced paved areas paired with durable, long-life materials.

options.name: Integrated Permeable & Low-Carbon Materials
options.description: Strategic use of permeable paving and materials selected for long-term environmental performance.

options.name: Minimum Code Amenities
options.description: Sidewalks, seating, and lighting provided to meet basic requirements.
cons: Limited comfort during heat or cold

options.name: Climate-Responsive Comfort
options.description: Shade, wind protection, and seating are intentionally located to extend seasonal use.
cons: Requires coordination with trees and structures

options.name: Year-Round Usability
options.description: Site design supports comfort across seasons through canopy, materials, and microclimate strategies.
